title: Copy-on-modify semantics
description: R vectors are copied when modified through a name with multiple bindings, which drives loop cost.
body: Use when reasoning about why growing structures in loops is slow in R. R uses copy-on-modify: a value is shared until a modification through one binding forces a copy. Growing a vector with c() rebinds and copies the whole thing each iteration, so the loop becomes quadratic. Do not assume in-place mutation. Actions: preallocate to final size or vectorize so copies do not accumulate. Validation signals include a benchmark that stays linear in n. Risk boundary: hidden copies inflate memory on large objects, not just time.
